seo.description: Turn large sets of reference images into one coherent hero shot. Use OpenCreator's Multi-Reference Image Generator to merge poses, props, styling and lighting from many examples into a single, production-ready visual.

hero.title: Multi-Reference Image Generator

items.question: What does the Mega Multi Reference Builder AI workflow do?
items.answer: This workflow helps you build large, organized visual reference boards with AI. It's a reference board and visual moodboard generator that combines many image and text references into coherent systems.

items.question: What kind of references should I gather before using this workflow?
items.answer: Collect screenshots, photos, sketches, and links that represent the direction you want—such as campaign routes, worldbuilding inspiration, character references, or brand territories. The more representative and well-chosen the inputs, the more useful the resulting boards.

items.question: How does AI help organize my references?
items.answer: The workflow groups, arranges, and harmonizes references into clear sections and visual flows based on your descriptions and the content of the inputs, so you spend less time manually arranging collages.

items.question: Who typically uses large-scale reference boards?
items.answer: Creative, brand, and art directors; film, game, and animation teams; agencies and studios; and educators or workshop leaders who need to organize large pools of visual inspiration into something shareable and easy to understand.

items.question: Can I export boards for collaboration and production use?
items.answer: Yes. The workflow supports exporting boards as images or PDFs so they can be shared with teams, clients, or vendors as alignment tools before production work begins.

items.question: How does pricing work?
items.answer: Each generated board consumes credits depending on how many references you include and how complex the layout needs are. Many teams start with smaller sets to validate structure, then scale up to larger boards once they're confident in the setup.
